Now, the seafood chain’s 37-year-old chief executive officer says he’s planning “the greatest comeback in the history of the restaurant industry.”
Before that, he worked with Fortress Investment Group as it prepared to acquire the Florida-based restaurant chain."},{"url":"https://www.seafoodsource.com/news/business-finance/red-lobster-names-new-ceo-will-close-23-more-restaurants","title":"Red Lobster names new CEO, will close 23 more restaurants | SeafoodSource","content":"# Red Lobster names new CEO, will close 23 more restaurants
Restaurant chain Red Lobster announced plans to close 23 more locations as part of its ongoing bankruptcy proceedings.
Additionally, the chain has appointed a new CEO to take over operations after a U.S. bankruptcy judge officially approves its sale.
Orlando, Florida, U.S.A.-based Red Lobster, which filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in May, is rejecting the leases of 23 additional locations by 31 August, CNN reported.
The company will have around 544 locations left across the U.S. and Canada after the bankruptcy proceedings, its new owner, Fortress Investment Group, said.
“The pattern of the earthquake [of units closing] is diminishing,” Pacific Management Consulting Group Founder and CEO John Gordon told SeafoodSource.
“Damola’s energy, leadership, and experience will be key to restoring Red Lobster’s status as an iconic and admired American brand.”
After staying with the same restaurant model and formula for many years, Red Lobster could develop a new prototype – such as a restaurant with a more substantial bar presence – that could elevate the quality of customer, rather than focusing on discounting, Gordon said.
Red Lobster claimed Thai Union exerted undue influence on the chain’s shrimp purchasing, while Thai Union claimed Red Lobster owes it USD 3.7 million (EUR 3.3 million) due to demand forecast discrepancies.
© 2026 Diversified Communications."},{"url":"https://www.fox6now.com/news/red-lobster-considering-more-restaurant-closures-ceo-says","title":"Red Lobster considering more restaurant closures, CEO says | FOX6 Milwaukee","content":"Red Lobster filed for bankruptcy in May 2024 after it racked up steep losses amid reduced sales and losses generated from an endless shrimp deal that was originally priced at $20.
Adamolekun was hired as CEO by the chain's new ownership in August 2024 after he led a restructuring effort at P.F.
Adamolekun told the Journal that once the company has dealt with struggling locations, Red Lobster could look to expand in upstate New York and New England, where it has a limited presence.
He's also considering franchise deals for international locations as well as selling more Red Lobster-branded products, like Cheddar Bay Biscuit mixes, through retail channels."}]}
